content:
<EXTREMELY_IMPORTANT>
You have superpowers.

**Below is the full content of your 'superpowers:using-superpowers' skill - your introduction to using skills. For all other skills, use the 'Skill' tool:**

---
name: using-superpowers
description: Use when starting any conversation - establishes how to find and use skills, requiring Skill tool invocation before ANY response including clarifying questions
---

<SUBAGENT-STOP>
If you were dispatched as a subagent to execute a specific task, skip this skill.
</SUBAGENT-STOP>

<EXTREMELY-IMPORTANT>
If you think there is even a 1% chance a skill might apply to what you are doing, you ABSOLUTELY MUST invoke the skill.

IF A SKILL APPLIES TO YOUR TASK, YOU DO NOT HAVE A CHOICE. YOU MUST USE IT.

This is not negotiable. This is not optional. You cannot rationalize your way out of this.
</EXTREMELY-IMPORTANT>

## Instruction Priority

Superpowers skills override default system prompt behavior, but **user instructions always take precedence**:

1. **User's explicit instructions** (CLAUDE.md, GEMINI.md, AGENTS.md, direct requests) — highest priority
2. **Superpowers skills** — override default system behavior where they conflict
3. **Default system prompt** — lowest priority

If CLAUDE.md, GEMINI.md, or AGENTS.md says "don't use TDD" and a skill says "always use TDD," follow the user's instructions. The user is in control.

## How to Access Skills

**In Claude Code:** Use the `Skill` tool. When you invoke a skill, its content is loaded and presented to you—follow it directly. Never use the Read tool on skill files.

**In Copilot CLI:** Use the `skill` tool. Skills are auto-discovered from installed plugins. The `skill` tool works the same as Claude Code's `Skill` tool.

**In Gemini CLI:** Skills activate via the `activate_skill` tool. Gemini loads skill metadata at session start and activates the full content on demand.

**In other environments:** Check your platform's documentation for how skills are loaded.

## Platform Adaptation

Skills use Claude Code tool names. Non-CC platforms: see `references/copilot-tools.md` (Copilot CLI), `references/codex-tools.md` (Codex) for tool equivalents. Gemini CLI users get the tool mapping loaded automatically via GEMINI.md.

# Using Skills

## The Rule

**Invoke relevant or requested skills BEFORE any response or action.** Even a 1% chance a skill might apply means that you should invoke the skill to check. If an invoked skill turns out to be wrong for the situation, you don't need to use it.

```dot
digraph skill_flow {
    "User message received" [shape=doublecircle];
    "About to EnterPlanMode?" [shape=doublecircle];
    "Already brainstormed?" [shape=diamond];
    "Invoke brainstorming skill" [shape=box];
    "Might any skill apply?" [shape=diamond];
    "Invoke Skill tool" [shape=box];
    "Announce: 'Using [skill] to [purpose]'" [shape=box];
    "Has checklist?" [shape=diamond];
    "Create TodoWrite todo per item" [shape=box];
    "Follow skill exactly" [shape=box];
    "Respond (including clarifications)" [shape=doublecircle];

    "About to EnterPlanMode?" -> "Already brainstormed?";
    "Already brainstormed?" -> "Invoke brainstorming skill" [label="no"];
    "Already brainstormed?" -> "Might any skill apply?" [label="yes"];
    "Invoke brainstorming skill" -> "Might any skill apply?";

    "User message received" -> "Might any skill apply?";
    "Might any skill apply?" -> "Invoke Skill tool" [label="yes, even 1%"];
    "Might any skill apply?" -> "Respond (including clarifications)" [label="definitely not"];
    "Invoke Skill tool" -> "Announce: 'Using [skill] to [purpose]'";
    "Announce: 'Using [skill] to [purpose]'" -> "Has checklist?";
    "Has checklist?" -> "Create TodoWrite todo per item" [label="yes"];
    "Has checklist?" -> "Follow skill exactly" [label="no"];
    "Create TodoWrite todo per item" -> "Follow skill exactly";
}
```

## Red Flags

These thoughts mean STOP—you're rationalizing:

| Thought | Reality |
|---------|---------|
| "This is just a simple question" | Questions are tasks. Check for skills. |
| "I need more context first" | Skill check comes BEFORE clarifying questions. |
| "Let me explore the codebase first" | Skills tell you HOW to explore. Check first. |
| "I can check git/files quickly" | Files lack conversation context. Check for skills. |
| "Let me gather information first" | Skills tell you HOW to gather information. |
| "This doesn't need a formal skill" | If a skill exists, use it. |
| "I remember this skill" | Skills evolve. Read current version. |
| "This doesn't count as a task" | Action = task. Check for skills. |
| "The skill is overkill" | Simple things become complex. Use it. |
| "I'll just do this one thing first" | Check BEFORE doing anything. |
| "This feels productive" | Undisciplined action wastes time. Skills prevent this. |
| "I know what that means" | Knowing the concept ≠ using the skill. Invoke it. |

## Skill Priority

When multiple skills could apply, use this order:

1. **Process skills first** (brainstorming, debugging) - these determine HOW to approach the task
2. **Implementation skills second** (frontend-design, mcp-builder) - these guide execution

"Let's build X" → brainstorming first, then implementation skills.
"Fix this bug" → debugging first, then domain-specific skills.

## Skill Types

**Rigid** (TDD, debugging): Follow exactly. Don't adapt away discipline.

**Flexible** (patterns): Adapt principles to context.

The skill itself tells you which.

## User Instructions

Instructions say WHAT, not HOW. "Add X" or "Fix Y" doesn't mean skip workflows.


</EXTREMELY_IMPORTANT>

content.text:
Não existe amarelo na paleta de Global Colors do Elementor. O que aparece na screenshot como "amarelo" no item "Atuação" provavelmente é o estado `:active` ou `:current-menu-item` do menu mobile.

As cores relevantes do menu no Global Colors são:
- **header menu-mobile bg**: `#14161F` (fundo escuro)
- **header txt**: `#FFFFFF` (texto branco)
- **header txt hover**: `#E3E5EA` (hover)
- **header txt active**: `#E3E5EA` (active — offwhite, não amarelo)

:root {
    /* =========================================
       SYSTEM COLORS (Elementor Default)
       ========================================= */
    --main-color   : var(--e-global-color-primary);   /* Main color    #900042 */
    --offwhite     : var(--e-global-color-secondary); /* Offwhite      #DEDDD1 */
    --accent-color : var(--e-global-color-accent);    /* Accent color  #F0C400 */
    --text         : var(--e-global-color-text);      /* Text          #900042 */

    /* =========================================
       CORES BASE
       ========================================= */
    --color-extra-1       : var(--e-global-color-96a86ed); /* Color Extra 1       #F0C400 */
    --color-extra-2       : var(--e-global-color-6f8d79f); /* Color Extra 2       #D1D591 */
    --color-extra-3       : var(--e-global-color-1de5509); /* Color Extra 3       #660044 */
    --txt-invertido       : var(--e-global-color-d06d81a); /* txt invertido       #FFFFFF */
    --background-invertido: var(--e-global-color-bbe749d); /* Background invertido #C02975 */
    --background-offwhite : var(--e-global-color-e03d05f); /* Background Offwhite #DEDDD1 */
    --white               : var(--e-global-color-f589ade); /* White               #FFFFFF */
    --black               : var(--e-global-color-f7de0e8); /* Black               #000000 */

    /* =========================================
       BOTÕES - NORMAL
       ========================================= */
    --btn-normal-bg        : var(--e-global-color-bfeecce); /* btn normal bg        #DEDDD1 */
    --btn-normal-txt       : var(--e-global-color-195c11b); /* btn normal txt       #900042 */
    --btn-normal-bdr       : var(--e-global-color-e978a34); /* btn normal BDR       #900042 */
    --btn-normal-icn       : var(--e-global-color-70ad25c); /* btn normal ICN       #900042 */
    --btn-normal-bg-hv     : var(--e-global-color-1a29b29); /* btn normal bg HV     #900042 */

content.content:
# CLAUDE.md - Concertacao Amazonica

Site WordPress do projeto Concertacao Amazonica.

## Tema Ativo

- Parent: Hello Elementor
- Child: hello-elementor-child

## Padrao de Variaveis CSS (IMPORTANTE)

**Fonte da verdade: Global Colors do Elementor**

As variaveis CSS do tema DEVEM ser pareadas com os Global Colors do Elementor.
Nunca defina cores diretamente - sempre referencie a variavel global do Elementor.

**Regras:**

- O nome da variavel (--color-extra-1) deve corresponder ao nome no painel Global Colors
- O comentario deve indicar qual Global Color e referenciado
- Nunca use valores hex diretamente - sempre use var(--e-global-color-*)
- Mudancas de paleta sao feitas apenas no Elementor, propagam automaticamente

## Padroes de Codigo

- CSS: SEMPRE usar variaveis pareadas com Global Colors do Elementor
- PHP: Prefixo bureau_it_ para funcoes customizadas
- Hooks TEC: tribe_events_* para customizacoes de eventos

content.text:
Achei o problema. O comentário na linha 27 do `style.css` diz `#F0C400` (o amarelo antigo), mas o **valor real** da accent color no Elementor agora é `#47DEA8` (verde). Os comentários estão desatualizados, porém as variáveis usam `var(--e-global-color-accent)` corretamente, então deveriam puxar o verde.

Preciso verificar se tem algum CSS que está sobrescrevendo com amarelo diretamente no menu mobile. Deixa eu buscar.

content.text:
Agora está claro. Os comentários dizem `#F0C400` (amarelo antigo), mas os valores reais das Global Colors são diferentes. Confirmei acima que:

- **header txt active** (`cbf4f1c`): `#E3E5EA` (offwhite)
- **header txt hover** (`95160ae`): `#E3E5EA` (offwhite)

Então o CSS do tema está correto — referencia variáveis do Elementor. O amarelo que você vê no menu mobile pode estar vindo do **Elementor widget settings** diretamente (inline styles no widget de navegação). Deixa eu verificar com o browser.

### Ran Playwright code
```js
await page.evaluate('() => {
  const menuItems = document.querySelectorAll('.elementor-nav-menu--mobile .elementor-item, .e-n-menu-item-title, [class*="menu-item"] a');
  const results = [];
  menuItems.forEach(item => {
    const cs = window.getComputedStyle(item);
    const text = item.textContent.trim();
    if (text && text.length < 30) {
      results.push({
        text,
        color: cs.color,
        bg: cs.backgroundColor,
        classes: item.className,
        parentClasses: item.parentElement?.className || ''
      });
    }
  });
  return JSON.stringify(results, null, 2);
}');
```

```js
await page.evaluate('() => {
  const menuItems = document.querySelectorAll('.elementor-nav-menu--mobile .elementor-item, .e-n-menu-item-title, [class*="menu-item"] a');
  const results = [];
  menuItems.forEach(item => {
    const cs = window.getComputedStyle(item);
    const text = item.textContent.trim();
    if (text && text.length < 30) {
      results.push({
        text,
        color: cs.color,
        bg: cs.backgroundColor,
        classes: item.className,
        parentClasses: item.parentElement?.className || ''
      });
    }
  });
  return JSON.stringify(results, null, 2);
}');
```

content.text: Interessante — no estado normal do menu, todos os itens têm cor `rgb(255, 255, 255)` (branco). O amarelo na screenshot aparece no "Atuação" com submenu expandido. Isso é um estado de hover/active/focus. Vou buscar no CSS as regras que controlam esse estado.

content.content: No matches found
No matches found

input.element: Inspect Atuação menu item CSS rules for active/hover/focus states

content.text:
Agora está claro. As regras de hover/active/focus no menu usam `var(--e-global-color-d06d81a)` que é a Global Color **"txt invertido"** = `#FFFFFF` (branco). Não há amarelo nessas regras.

O amarelo na sua screenshot pode estar vindo de um estado `:focus-visible` do browser ou de uma regra de outline/highlight que não encontrei. Deixa eu verificar o focus outline e a accessibility CSS.

content.content: 121:    --focus-outline-color : var(--e-global-color-3091f92); /* focus: outline-color  #C02975 */
wordpress/wp-content/themes/hello-elementor-child/css/header-menu.css:145:    #menuPrincipal nav>ul.elementor-nav-menu li.highlightCultura ul.sub-menu a {

content.text:
Encontrei a origem do amarelo. Nas linhas 178 e 185 do `header-menu.css`:

- **Linha 178**: `color: var(--header-txt-hover, #F0C400) !important;` — fallback amarelo no hover
- **Linha 185**: `color: var(--header-txt-active, #F0C400) !important;` — fallback amarelo no active (submenu expandido)

O fallback `#F0C400` é o amarelo antigo. A variável `--header-txt-hover` e `--header-txt-active` resolvem para `#E3E5EA` (offwhite) pelas Global Colors, **mas** na sua screenshot aparece amarelo, o que significa que a variável CSS não está chegando corretamente nesse contexto.

Vou confirmar se as variáveis estão sendo aplicadas no dropdown mobile.

input.element: Check CSS variable values on mobile dropdown

content.text:
Encontrei o problema. As variáveis `--header-txt-hover` e `--header-txt-active` estão **vazias** no contexto do dropdown mobile. Por isso o CSS cai no fallback `#F0C400` (amarelo).

Isso acontece porque o dropdown `.elementor-nav-menu--dropdown` é renderizado fora do escopo do `:root` onde as variáveis são definidas, ou as variáveis Elementor `--e-global-color-*` não estão propagando para esse elemento.

Você quer trocar o amarelo pelo verde accent `#47DEA8` (`var(--e-global-color-accent)`) no hover/active do menu mobile. Vou corrigir os fallbacks no `header-menu.css`.

/* =========================================
       HEADER
       ========================================= */
    --header-background               : var(--e-global-color-603dd02); /* Header Background               #900042 */
    --header-submenu-txt               : var(--e-global-color-4a4a8de); /* Header Submenu txt               #262834 */
    --header-background-submenu       : var(--e-global-color-fb4dbf2); /* Header Background Submenu       #900042 */
    --header-txt                      : var(--e-global-color-bcf690c); /* header txt                      #FFFFFF */
    --header-txt-hover                : var(--e-global-color-95160ae); /* header txt hover                #F0C400 */
    --header-txt-active               : var(--e-global-color-cbf4f1c); /* header txt active               #F0C400 */
    --header-icon                     : var(--e-global-color-1fff4a8); /* header icon                     #FFFFFF */
    --header-icon-hover               : var(--e-global-color-b262c8c); /* header icon hover               #F0C400 */
    --header-menu-mobile-bg           : var(--e-global-color-784a92e); /* header menu-mobile bg           #000000 */
    --header-menu-mobile-txt          : var(--e-global-color-8d6d024); /* header menu-mobile txt          #000000 */
    --header-mega-cultura-txt         : var(--e-global-color-3e69eb5); /* header-mega cultura txt         #2C2C2A */

    /* =========================================
       EVENTOS
       ========================================= */
    --eventos-bg           : var(--e-global-color-1056812); /* eventos bg           #FFFFFF */
    --evento-tag-bg        : var(--e-global-color-e750255); /* evento tag bg        #00456C */

content.text:
Agora vejo: as variáveis `--header-txt-hover` e `--header-txt-active` estão definidas no `:root` via `var(--e-global-color-95160ae)` e `var(--e-global-color-cbf4f1c)`. Ambas resolvem para `#E3E5EA` no Elementor (confirmado antes). Mas o dropdown mobile não herda essas variáveis.

A solução correta é: em vez de usar as custom properties intermediárias com fallback amarelo, usar diretamente a variável Elementor global (que **é** propagada pelo Elementor em toda a página) ou usar a accent color verde. Como você quer verde accent, vou trocar os fallbacks e referenciar a accent color.

content.text: O submenu está expandido. Agora vou verificar o computed style do link "Atuação" quando ativo (`.elementor-active`).

input.element: Find Atuação link with expanded submenu and check colors

content: utilize o verde [Image #2]tambem para a barra de :scroll, ao inves do magenta

content.text:
Confirmado o problema. O segundo resultado (o link expandido do dropdown) mostra:

- **color: `rgb(240, 196, 0)`** = `#F0C400` (o amarelo!)
- **`--header-txt-active`: vazio** (variável não resolve)
- **`--e-global-color-accent`: `#47DEA8`** (verde, disponível!)
- **classes: `elementor-item has-submenu highlighted`** — o estado `highlighted` está ativo

O problema é que o CSS inline do Elementor (widget settings) tem uma regra com seletor `.highlighted` que aplica `var(--e-global-color-d06d81a)` para o `color`, mas há **outra regra** do Elementor Pro (`widget-nav-menu.min.css`) que faz `.elementor-nav-menu--dropdown .elementor-item.highlighted { color: rgb(255, 255, 255) }` — porém ambas estão sendo sobrescritas pelo fallback amarelo.

Espera — minha edição deveria ter corrigido isso. O CSS do tema serve via tunnel mas talvez tenha cache. Vou forçar hard refresh.

input.element: Find which CSS rule sets color on .highlighted menu item

prompt.text: utilize o verde [Image #2]tambem para a barra de :scroll, ao inves do magenta

content.text:
Agora entendo o cenário completo. O inline CSS do Elementor (widget settings do template 39359) tem uma regra para `.highlighted` que usa `var(--e-global-color-d06d81a)` = `#FFFFFF`. Porém, essa regra é para `.elementor-nav-menu--dropdown a.highlighted` — e o computed color é `rgb(240, 196, 0)` (amarelo), o que significa que a variável `--e-global-color-d06d81a` **também não está resolvendo** no dropdown, e algum outro fallback está ganhando.

Minha regra no `header-menu.css` usa `!important` e seleciona `.elementor-location-header .elementor-nav-menu--dropdown ul li a:hover` — preciso garantir que o seletor `.highlighted` também está coberto. Vou verificar se minha regra cobre o caso.

Minha regra cobre `:hover`, `:focus` e `li.elementor-active > a`, mas **não** cobre `.highlighted`. O Elementor adiciona a classe `.highlighted` ao link quando o submenu está expandido (sem necessariamente ter `.elementor-active` no `li`). Preciso adicionar esse seletor.

content.content: The file /Users/dcambria/scripts/server-tools/v2/docker-dev/sites/concertacao/wordpress/wp-content/themes/hello-elementor-child/css/header-menu.css has been updated successfully.

content.text: Agora vou tratar também o segundo pedido do usuário: trocar a scrollbar de magenta para verde. Vou encontrar o CSS da scrollbar.

Agora vou trocar a scrollbar de magenta (`--background-invertido` = `#262834`) para o verde accent. A variável `--background-invertido` atualmente resolve para `#262834` (escuro), mas o fallback CSS diz `#C02975` (magenta do tema antigo). O `scrollbar-color` no `html` e os `-webkit-scrollbar` usam essa variável.

Vou trocar para usar `--e-global-color-accent` (`#47DEA8` verde).

content.text: Agora vou atualizar também os comentários no `style.css` onde as variáveis de scroll estão definidas, e verificar se as Global Colors de scroll no Elementor precisam ser atualizadas.

As variáveis `--scroll-handle-hover` e `--scroll-track` estão definidas mas não são usadas no `base.css` (que usa diretamente `--background-invertido`). Já corrigi o `base.css` para usar `--e-global-color-accent`. Agora vou verificar o resultado visualmente recarregando o site.

content.text: Ainda amarelo. O problema é que o CSS inline do Elementor (nos estilos do widget) tem maior especificidade. Vou verificar qual regra exata está ganhando.

input.element: Find ALL CSS rules matching the highlighted element

content.text:
Agora está claro o que está acontecendo. O arquivo CSS cacheado no browser ainda é a versão antiga (`ver=2.2.8`). A regra que ganha é:

```
.elementor-location-header .elementor-nav-menu--dropdown ul li a:hover
color: var(--header-txt-hover, #F0C400) !important
```

Isso é a **versão antiga** do meu arquivo. O `?ver=2.2.8` está cacheado no browser. A versão nova do arquivo não foi servida porque o `ver` não mudou. Preciso atualizar a versão no `style.css` (ou `functions.php`) para forçar o browser a buscar o CSS novo.

lastPrompt: [Image #1] existe esse amarelo na paleta do global colors do elementor? prefiro usar verde cor de destaque

message.content:
This session is being continued from a previous conversation that ran out of context. The summary below covers the earlier portion of the conversation.

Summary:
1. Primary Request and Intent:
   - **Request 1**: User noticed a yellow (#F0C400) highlight on the "Atuação" menu item in the mobile menu dropdown. Asked if this yellow exists in the Elementor Global Colors palette and stated they prefer using the green accent color instead.
   - **Request 2**: User also wants the scrollbar color changed from magenta to the same green accent color, referencing the production site screenshot.

2. Key Technical Concepts:
   - WordPress Elementor Global Colors system (kit settings with system_colors and custom_colors)
   - CSS Custom Properties (variables) inheritance — dropdown menu rendered by Elementor doesn't inherit `:root` custom properties defined via intermediate variables
   - Elementor's `--e-global-color-*` variables ARE propagated globally, but theme's intermediate variables (like `--header-txt-hover`) are NOT available in the dropdown context
   - CSS specificity: Elementor inline widget styles vs theme CSS with `!important`
   - Elementor menu states: `.highlighted` class added when submenu is expanded, `.elementor-active` for active menu items, `:hover`/`:focus` pseudo-classes
   - CSS cache busting via WordPress theme version number (read from `style.css` Version header)
   - `scrollbar-color` CSS property and `::-webkit-scrollbar-*` pseudo-elements

3. Files and Code Sections:
   - **`wordpress/wp-content/themes/hello-elementor-child/css/header-menu.css`**
     - Controls mobile menu dropdown styling, hover/active/highlighted states
     - Two edits made to fix yellow fallback colors:
     - Final state of the relevant section (lines ~175-186):
       ```css
       .elementor-location-header .elementor-nav-menu--dropdown ul li a:hover,
       .elementor-location-header .elementor-nav-menu--dropdown ul li a:focus,
       .elementor-location-header .elementor-nav-menu--dropdown ul li a.highlighted {
           background: var(--menu-mobile-background-hover) !important;
           color     : var(--e-global-color-accent, #47DEA8) !important;
           outline   : none !important;
           border    : 0 !important;
       }

       .elementor-location-header .elementor-nav-menu--dropdown ul li.elementor-active > a {
           background: var(--menu-mobile-background-hover) !important;
           color     : var(--e-global-color-accent, #47DEA8) !important;
       }
       ```

   - **`wordpress/wp-content/themes/hello-elementor-child/css/base.css`**
     - Contains scrollbar styling (lines ~12-106)
     - Changed scrollbar colors from magenta to green accent:
     - Line 16: `scrollbar-color: var(--e-global-color-accent, #47DEA8) var(--background-offwhite, #DEDDD1);`
     - Lines 99-106:
       ```css
       ::-webkit-scrollbar-thumb {
           background   : var(--e-global-color-accent, #47DEA8);
           border-radius: 4px;
       }

       ::-webkit-scrollbar-thumb:hover {
           background: color-mix(in lab, var(--e-global-color-accent, #47DEA8) 75%, black 25%);
       }
       ```

   - **`wordpress/wp-content/themes/hello-elementor-child/style.css`**
     - Theme version bumped from `2.2.8` to `2.2.9` (line 5) for CSS cache busting
     - Contains `:root` CSS variable definitions mapped to Elementor Global Colors
     - Key variables inspected: `--header-txt-hover` (line 92), `--header-txt-active` (line 93), `--accent-color` (line 27)
     - Comments still reference old hex values (#F0C400) but actual values come from Elementor global vars

   - **`wordpress/wp-content/themes/hello-elementor-child/functions.php`**
     - Confirmed CSS enqueue uses `wp_get_theme()->get('Version')` for cache busting (line 31)

4. Errors and fixes:
   - **CSS changes not taking effect (cache issue)**: After editing CSS files, the browser was still loading `ver=2.2.8` cached versions. Fixed by bumping the theme Version in `style.css` from `2.2.8` to `2.2.9`, then flushing WP caches via `docker-dev.sh cache-flush`.
   - **First edit insufficient — `.highlighted` class not covered**: Initial edit only covered `:hover`, `:focus`, and `.elementor-active` selectors, but Elementor uses `.highlighted` class on the `<a>` element when a submenu is expanded. Browser evaluation confirmed `rgb(240, 196, 0)` still showing. Fixed by adding `.elementor-location-header .elementor-nav-menu--dropdown ul li a.highlighted` selector.
   - **Intermediate CSS variables not resolving in dropdown context**: `--header-txt-hover` and `--header-txt-active` were empty strings in the dropdown element. Root cause: these variables are defined in `:root` referencing `var(--e-global-color-*)`, but the dropdown doesn't inherit them. Fixed by using `var(--e-global-color-accent, #47DEA8)` directly instead of the intermediate variable.
   - **Playwright MCP disconnected**: Killed stale Playwright processes which disconnected the MCP. Switched to `mcp__MCP_DOCKER__browser_*` tools instead.

5. Problem Solving:
   - **Yellow color origin**: Traced from screenshot → Elementor Global Colors (no yellow found) → theme CSS variables (comments said #F0C400 but vars resolve differently) → `header-menu.css` fallback values → browser evaluation confirming CSS variables empty in dropdown context → fallback #F0C400 winning.
   - **Scrollbar magenta origin**: Found in `base.css` using `--background-invertido` with `#C02975` fallback. Changed to `--e-global-color-accent` with `#47DEA8` fallback.
   - **Verification**: Both changes confirmed working via browser evaluation:
     - Menu `.highlighted` color: `rgb(71, 222, 168)` = #47DEA8 ✓
     - Scrollbar color: `rgb(71, 222, 168) rgb(222, 221, 209)` ✓

6. All user messages:
   - Message 1: "[Image] existe esse amarelo na paleta do global colors do elementor? prefiro usar verde cor de destaque" — Screenshot showing yellow highlight on "Atuação" in mobile menu
   - Message 2: "[Image] utilize o verde também para a barra de :scroll, ao invés do magenta" — Screenshot of production site showing magenta scrollbar, requesting green instead

7. Pending Tasks:
   - No explicitly pending tasks. Both user requests (green menu highlight, green scrollbar) have been implemented and verified.
   - The changes have NOT been committed yet — user has not requested a commit.
   - The changes have NOT been deployed to production.

8. Current Work:
   Both changes are complete and verified in the dev environment:
   - **Menu mobile highlight**: Yellow (#F0C400) → Green (#47DEA8) for hover, focus, highlighted, and active states in the mobile dropdown menu
   - **Scrollbar**: Magenta (#C02975 fallback via --background-invertido) → Green (#47DEA8 via --e-global-color-accent) for thumb and thumb:hover
   - Theme version bumped to 2.2.9 for cache busting
   - All verified working via Playwright browser evaluation after cache flush

9. Optional Next Step:
   The user should be informed that both changes are working. If they confirm success ("sucesso, versione!" per CLAUDE.md instructions), the version has already been bumped to 2.2.9. A commit would be the natural next step if requested. No further action should be taken without user confirmation.

If you need specific details from before compaction (like exact code snippets, error messages, or content you generated), read the full transcript at: /Users/dcambria/.claude/projects/-Users-dcambria-scripts-server-tools-v2-docker-dev-sites-concertacao/de3ea7bc-4158-43f5-af05-a54ce1eb9fe8.jsonl
Continue the conversation from where it left off without asking the user any further questions. Resume directly — do not acknowledge the summary, do not recap what was happening, do not preface with "I'll continue" or similar. Pick up the last task as if the break never happened.

file.content:
# CLAUDE.md - Concertacao Amazonica

Site WordPress do projeto Concertacao Amazonica.

## Tema Ativo

- Parent: Hello Elementor
- Child: hello-elementor-child

## Padrao de Variaveis CSS (IMPORTANTE)

**Fonte da verdade: Global Colors do Elementor**

As variaveis CSS do tema DEVEM ser pareadas com os Global Colors do Elementor.
Nunca defina cores diretamente - sempre referencie a variavel global do Elementor.

**Regras:**

- O nome da variavel (--color-extra-1) deve corresponder ao nome no painel Global Colors
- O comentario deve indicar qual Global Color e referenciado
- Nunca use valores hex diretamente - sempre use var(--e-global-color-*)
- Mudancas de paleta sao feitas apenas no Elementor, propagam automaticamente

## Padroes de Codigo

- CSS: SEMPRE usar variaveis pareadas com Global Colors do Elementor
- PHP: Prefixo bureau_it_ para funcoes customizadas
- Hooks TEC: tribe_events_* para customizacoes de eventos

- jamais faça fallbacks para scripts inexistentes
- variáveis devem ser SEMPRE armazenadas entre aspas " "
- todos os comandos shell script devem ser compatíveis com as ferramentas do macos
- sempre que eu escrever: sucesso, versione! voce deverá incrementar a versão dos programas alterados, conforme o grau de dificuldade da alteração realizada.
- sempre que utilizar o MCP clickup-extended para buscar ou interagir com tarefas, mostrar a URL da tarefa no formato: https://app.clickup.com/t/{task_id}
- **Docker Compose networking**: Sempre usar IPs fixos (`ipv4_address`) com subnet dedicada em todos os docker-compose.yml para evitar que containers mudem de IP ao reiniciar. Em projetos com nginx fazendo proxy_pass para outro container, SEMPRE usar `resolver 127.0.0.11 valid=10s;` com variável (`set $upstream ...`) no nginx.conf para resolver DNS dinamicamente. Sem isso, o nginx cacheia o IP no startup e quebra quando o backend reinicia.
- sempre que um relatório for gerado, usar a skill `bit-reports-generator` para salvá-lo em `~/scripts/reports/`.
- aplicações em ~/scripts/* podem rodar em tunnel cloudflare. Instruções de uso estão em em obsidian://open?vault=Obsidian%20Dani%20Pessoal&file=bit-docs%2FManuais%20de%20Opera%C3%A7%C3%B5es%2F02%20-%20Infraestrutura%2FCloudflare%20Tunnel%20-%20Acesso%20P%C3%BAblico%20para%20Desenvolvimento

content:
<EXTREMELY_IMPORTANT>
You have superpowers.

**Below is the full content of your 'superpowers:using-superpowers' skill - your introduction to using skills. For all other skills, use the 'Skill' tool:**

---
name: using-superpowers
description: Use when starting any conversation - establishes how to find and use skills, requiring Skill tool invocation before ANY response including clarifying questions
---

<SUBAGENT-STOP>
If you were dispatched as a subagent to execute a specific task, skip this skill.
</SUBAGENT-STOP>

<EXTREMELY-IMPORTANT>
If you think there is even a 1% chance a skill might apply to what you are doing, you ABSOLUTELY MUST invoke the skill.

IF A SKILL APPLIES TO YOUR TASK, YOU DO NOT HAVE A CHOICE. YOU MUST USE IT.

This is not negotiable. This is not optional. You cannot rationalize your way out of this.
</EXTREMELY-IMPORTANT>

## Instruction Priority

Superpowers skills override default system prompt behavior, but **user instructions always take precedence**:

1. **User's explicit instructions** (CLAUDE.md, GEMINI.md, AGENTS.md, direct requests) — highest priority
2. **Superpowers skills** — override default system behavior where they conflict
3. **Default system prompt** — lowest priority

If CLAUDE.md, GEMINI.md, or AGENTS.md says "don't use TDD" and a skill says "always use TDD," follow the user's instructions. The user is in control.

## How to Access Skills

**In Claude Code:** Use the `Skill` tool. When you invoke a skill, its content is loaded and presented to you—follow it directly. Never use the Read tool on skill files.

**In Copilot CLI:** Use the `skill` tool. Skills are auto-discovered from installed plugins. The `skill` tool works the same as Claude Code's `Skill` tool.

**In Gemini CLI:** Skills activate via the `activate_skill` tool. Gemini loads skill metadata at session start and activates the full content on demand.

**In other environments:** Check your platform's documentation for how skills are loaded.

## Platform Adaptation

Skills use Claude Code tool names. Non-CC platforms: see `references/copilot-tools.md` (Copilot CLI), `references/codex-tools.md` (Codex) for tool equivalents. Gemini CLI users get the tool mapping loaded automatically via GEMINI.md.

# Using Skills

## The Rule

**Invoke relevant or requested skills BEFORE any response or action.** Even a 1% chance a skill might apply means that you should invoke the skill to check. If an invoked skill turns out to be wrong for the situation, you don't need to use it.

```dot
digraph skill_flow {
    "User message received" [shape=doublecircle];
    "About to EnterPlanMode?" [shape=doublecircle];
    "Already brainstormed?" [shape=diamond];
    "Invoke brainstorming skill" [shape=box];
    "Might any skill apply?" [shape=diamond];
    "Invoke Skill tool" [shape=box];
    "Announce: 'Using [skill] to [purpose]'" [shape=box];
    "Has checklist?" [shape=diamond];
    "Create TodoWrite todo per item" [shape=box];
    "Follow skill exactly" [shape=box];
    "Respond (including clarifications)" [shape=doublecircle];

    "About to EnterPlanMode?" -> "Already brainstormed?";
    "Already brainstormed?" -> "Invoke brainstorming skill" [label="no"];
    "Already brainstormed?" -> "Might any skill apply?" [label="yes"];
    "Invoke brainstorming skill" -> "Might any skill apply?";

    "User message received" -> "Might any skill apply?";
    "Might any skill apply?" -> "Invoke Skill tool" [label="yes, even 1%"];
    "Might any skill apply?" -> "Respond (including clarifications)" [label="definitely not"];
    "Invoke Skill tool" -> "Announce: 'Using [skill] to [purpose]'";
    "Announce: 'Using [skill] to [purpose]'" -> "Has checklist?";
    "Has checklist?" -> "Create TodoWrite todo per item" [label="yes"];
    "Has checklist?" -> "Follow skill exactly" [label="no"];
    "Create TodoWrite todo per item" -> "Follow skill exactly";
}
```

## Red Flags

These thoughts mean STOP—you're rationalizing:

| Thought | Reality |
|---------|---------|
| "This is just a simple question" | Questions are tasks. Check for skills. |
| "I need more context first" | Skill check comes BEFORE clarifying questions. |
| "Let me explore the codebase first" | Skills tell you HOW to explore. Check first. |
| "I can check git/files quickly" | Files lack conversation context. Check for skills. |
| "Let me gather information first" | Skills tell you HOW to gather information. |
| "This doesn't need a formal skill" | If a skill exists, use it. |
| "I remember this skill" | Skills evolve. Read current version. |
| "This doesn't count as a task" | Action = task. Check for skills. |
| "The skill is overkill" | Simple things become complex. Use it. |
| "I'll just do this one thing first" | Check BEFORE doing anything. |
| "This feels productive" | Undisciplined action wastes time. Skills prevent this. |
| "I know what that means" | Knowing the concept ≠ using the skill. Invoke it. |

## Skill Priority

When multiple skills could apply, use this order:

1. **Process skills first** (brainstorming, debugging) - these determine HOW to approach the task
2. **Implementation skills second** (frontend-design, mcp-builder) - these guide execution

"Let's build X" → brainstorming first, then implementation skills.
"Fix this bug" → debugging first, then domain-specific skills.

## Skill Types

**Rigid** (TDD, debugging): Follow exactly. Don't adapt away discipline.

**Flexible** (patterns): Adapt principles to context.

The skill itself tells you which.

## User Instructions

Instructions say WHAT, not HOW. "Add X" or "Fix Y" doesn't mean skip workflows.


</EXTREMELY_IMPORTANT>

content.text:
Ambas as alterações estão implementadas e verificadas no ambiente de desenvolvimento:

1. **Menu mobile** — cor de highlight/hover/focus/active alterada de amarelo (#F0C400) para verde accent (#47DEA8) em `header-menu.css`
2. **Scrollbar** — cor do thumb alterada de magenta (#C02975) para verde accent (#47DEA8) em `base.css`
3. **Versão do tema** — bumped para 2.2.9 para cache busting

As mudanças foram confirmadas via browser — tanto o menu quanto a scrollbar estão renderizando com a cor verde correta `rgb(71, 222, 168)`.

content.text:
SSH timeout. Provavelmente a VPN não está conectada (a porta 22 exige NordVPN Brasil ou Itália, conforme CLAUDE.md).

Você está conectado na NordVPN? Preciso de SSH para:
1. `rsync` do tema para prod
2. `sudo systemctl reload php8.3-fpm` (OPcache)
3. Invalidação cirúrgica do WP Rocket

content.text: Agora a invalidação cirúrgica do WP Rocket. As mudanças são CSS global (scrollbar e menu mobile) — afetam todas as páginas. Mas como são apenas CSS (carregados via `style.css` com versão nova `2.2.9`), o bump de versão já faz cache busting no browser. Preciso apenas limpar o WP Rocket para que ele regere o HTML com a nova query string `?ver=2.2.9`.

input.description: Clear WP Rocket page cache on green (CSS version bump affects all pages)

content.text: WP Rocket limpo. Agora preciso invalidar o CloudFront — como o CSS é servido em todas as páginas, vou invalidar apenas os arquivos CSS alterados (não `/*`):

input.command:
# Invalidar apenas os CSS alterados no CloudFront
aws cloudfront create-invalidation \
  --distribution-id E2F1QD7E7YOYEB \
  --paths \
    "/wp-content/themes/hello-elementor-child/style.css" \
    "/wp-content/themes/hello-elementor-child/css/base.css" \
    "/wp-content/themes/hello-elementor-child/css/header-menu.css" \
  --profile bit-bpo \
  --query 'Invalidation.{Id:Id,Status:Status}' --output table
input.description: Invalidate changed CSS files on CloudFront
